observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to duly recognize the monthly depreciation of their Road Networks to reflect its correct carrying amounts as of year then ended.
11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the BAC Chairman to invite Observers to enhance transparency in all stages of the procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
16. Require and supervise the Inventory Committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of PPE and update and submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor; and
17. Require the General Services Office to maintain Property Cards to reconcile their records on a regular monthly basis with the Accounting Office.

18. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor create task force to negotiate with the previous administration of the Municipality to turn over the heavy equipment and settle the loans with the lending institution. Upon proper turned over, the accounting office, general services office and other concerned offices can make the appropriate reporting and recording of the loans and the proper accounts of the heavy equipment may be recorded and accounted for in the agency’s Books of Accounts in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
a. Require and supervise the Inventory Committee of the municipality
to conduct the physical count of PPE and update and submit the
RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor; and
b. Require the General Services Office to maintain Property Cards to
reconcile their records on a regular monthly basis with the
Accounting Office.

We reiterated to recommend that Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to duly recognize the annual depreciation of their
Road Networks to reflect its correct carrying amounts as of year then
ended.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the following:
a. Submit copy of the MOA by and between MPW-BARMM and the
Municipality Government to the Office of the Auditor;
b. Ensure the submission of copies of all other MOA/contracts of fund
transfers from Other Government Offices/NGOs within five (5)
working days from perfection thereof pursuant to COA Circular No.
2009-001 to facilitate review and technical evaluation of the
infrastructure projects;
c. Establish the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for each
fund transfer; and
d. Immediate submit all un-submitted reports, if any, pertaining to the
aforementioned projects to the Office of Auditor;

We reiterated to recommend that Local Chief Executive require:
a. The members of the LDRRMC to include in the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) all
unexpended balances of local DRRMF; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
   i. Disclose all unobligated balances of local DRRMF in the Notes
   to Financial Statements using the prescribed format (Annex D
   of aforementioned COA Circular); and
   ii. Prepare and submit regularly on a monthly basis the Report on
   Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
   Management Fund, duly noted by the MDRRM Officer.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive enforce the due compliance
with the requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2013-004,
specifically the following:
a. At the beginning of the year, the SA/ATL should be provided with a
list of all-on-going government PPAs and those that are to be
implemented during the year;
b. Provide the ATL with a monthly “Project Status” based on the data
of the monthly monitoring report of PPAs; and
c. Inform the SA/ATL within 10 days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity with
appropriate project signboards and/or public notices.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly RCI, ROD, RCD and
other related financial transaction documents regularly at the end of
the month to the Municipal Accountant; and
b. The Municipal Accountant, thereafter, shall submit to the Office of
the Audit Team Leader the monthly RCI, ROD and RCD within the
first 10 days of the ensuing month and the quarterly interim financial
statements not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing quarter for
audit and verification.

We recommended that Management conduct an orientation on GAD
among concerned personnel, ensure that proper budgeting, accounting and
reporting thereof are duly complied with and utilized according to its
intended purpose.

We recommended that the Management comply with the submission to the
Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/Contracts and Acceptance and
Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement of goods within five (5)
working days from the execution thereof and within 24 hours from acceptance of
goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

We recommended that Management comply with the submission of Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption and complete documentary requirements related
thereto of government motor transportations in pursuance to Section 361 of
Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor enjoin the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that required
documents are prepared and submitted prior to payment of salaries and wages
of laborers/workers.

2. We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoins the regular monitoring and review of their collection performance efficiency on real Property and Business Taxes to ensure performance and high revenue generation.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require:
3. The members of the LDRRMC to include in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) all unexpended balances of local DRRMF;
The Municipal Accountant to:
4. Disclose all unobligated balances of local DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed format (Annex D of aforementioned COA Circular); and
5. Prepare and submit regularly on a monthly basis the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund, duly noted by the MDRRM Officer.

6. We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to regularly monitor their disbursement system and to adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.

7. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and/or the General Services Officer to furnish the Auditor of the AIR within 24 hours after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU Personnel concerned on all consumable items so that a timely inspection can be made by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Accounting Office to:
8. Require the complete filling up of DVs, indicate the JEVs number specifically in its Box D to indicate preparation thereof; and
9. Ensure that it form part as an attachment to DVs to be submitted to the Auditor’s Office with proper letter of transmittal.

10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the BAC Chairman and/or BAC Secretariat to assist in the preparation and consolidation of PPMPs to APP and furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin:
12. The Municipal Accountant to ensure the complete supporting documents required under RA 9184 and its Revised IRR shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor regardless of amount;
13. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to observe faithfully the guidelines on the procurements of goods and services provided under existing rules and regulations.
14. All other municipal officials concerned to faithfully adhere to the provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in all stages of the procurement process.

15. We recommended that Management submit the documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other existing laws, rules and regulations to settle our audit suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Honorable Mayor:
19. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended LDRRM Fund which represents the Quick Response Fund (QRF) from General Fund to Special Trust Fund.
20. Require the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General Fund and Trust Fund.
21. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010).
22. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1.Require and supervise the Inventory Committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor; and
2.Require the General Services Office to maintain Property Cards to reconcile their records on a regular monthly basis with the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor coordinate with the previous administration of the Municipality to submit reports and documents pertaining to the loan granted to the municipality, if any. Then turn over the equipment purchased from the proceeds of the loan so that the accounting office, general services office and other concerned offices can make the appropriate reporting and recording of the loan and the heavy equipment and proper recording and accounting in the Books of Accounts can be made thereof in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.

1.4 We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to duly recognize the monthly depreciation of their Road
Networks to reflect its correct carrying amounts as of year then ended.

2.6 We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoins the regular
monitoring and review of their collection performance efficiency on real
Property and Business Taxes to ensure performance and high revenue
generation.

3.6 We recommended that Local Chief Executive require:
a) The members of the LDRRMC to include in the LDRRMFIP all
unexpended balances of local DRRMF;
b) The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Disclose all unobligated balances of local DRRMF in the Notes to
Financial Statements using the prescribed format (Annex D of
aforementioned COA Circular); and
ii. Prepare and submit regularly on a monthly basis the Report on
Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF, duly noted by the MDRRM
Officer.

4.4 We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Treasurer and Accountant to regularly monitor their disbursement system
and to adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its
future operations.

5.5 We recommended that Honorable Mayor enjoin the Municipal Treasurer
and/or the General Services Officer to furnish the Auditor of the AIR
within 24 hours after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU Personnel
concerned on all consumable items so that a timely inspection can be made
by the Audit Team.

6.7 We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the Accounting Office
to:
a. Require the complete filling up of DVs, indicate the JEVs number
specifically in its Box D to indicate preparation thereof; and
b. Ensure that it form part as an attachment to DVs to be submitted to
the Auditor’s Office with proper letter of transmittal.

7.7 We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the BAC Chairman
and/or BAC Secretariat to assist in the preparation and consolidation of
PPMPs to APP and furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor.

8.5 We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the BAC Chairman
and/or BAC Secretariat to invite Observers to enhance transparency in all
stages of the procurement process.

9.7 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor enjoin:
a. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting
documents required under RA 9184 and its Revised IRR shall be
submitted to the Office of the Auditor regardless of amount;
b. The BAC to observe faithfully the guidelines on the procurements of
goods and services provided under existing rules and regulations.
c. All other municipal officials concerned to faithfully adhere to the
provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in all stages of the
procurement process.

10.4 We recommended that Management submit the documents required
under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other existing
laws, rules and regulations to settle our audit suspensions pursuant to
Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor create task force to negotiate with the previous administration of the Municipality to turn over the heavy equipment and settle the loans with the lending institution. Upon proper turned over, the accounting office, general services office and other concerned offices can make the appropriate reporting and recording of the loans and the proper accounts of the heavy equipment may be recorded and accounted for in the agency’s Books of Accounts in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.

We recommended that the following measures:
4.Submit Supplemental Budget and Supplemental Investment Program with the approved Sangguniang Bayan Resolutions/Ordinances;
5.The Municipal Accountant to properly record and prepare separate account for the BAYANIHAN GRANTS in accordance with the generally accepted accounting rules and regulations; and
6.Municipal Treasurer must stamped “PAID” all Disbursement Vouchers and all its supporting documents pertaining to the BAYANIHAN GRANTS to avoid repetition of payments of expenses.

We recommended that Management:
7.Refrain from granting cash advances without first liquidating the previous cash advance; and
8.Ensure that the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 be properly complied with.

We reiterated and recommended that Management submit Contract of Service and Job Descriptions/Specifications of contractual or casual employees and observe the following measures:
9.Consider and limit the hiring of job orders to extremely necessary work assignment to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenses by the municipal government; and
10.Attached to the Daily Wage Payroll copy of the Contract of Service stipulated therein the specific purpose, work assignment and the specific duties and responsibilities of the job order employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Reports of Collections and Deposits for the calendar year 2020 for the funds received and collected together with the duplicate copy of the official receipt and deposits to validate the total collections of revenues presented in the financial statements for the end of the year.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor and/or the Municipal Accountant require the responsible/liable persons to submit the required documents or lacking supporting papers within 90 days from receipt of NS, otherwise, such suspensions will become disallowance and settlement thereof will be immediately enforced upon issuance of the Certificate of Execution by withholding the salaries or other claims due the persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and executory.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to post a tarpaulin signboard which must be suitably framed outdoor display at the project location or its activities with all the information as provided in Annex A pursuant to paragraph 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor and/or the Municipal Accountant require the responsible/liable persons to submit the required documents or lacking supporting papers within 90 days from receipt of NS, otherwise, such suspensions will become disallowance and settlement thereof will be immediately enforced upon issuance of the Certificate of Execution by withholding the salaries or other claims due the persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and executory.

We recommended that Honorable Mayor:
16.Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended LDRRM Fund which represents the Quick Response Fund (QRF) from General Fund to Special Trust Fund.
17.Require the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General Fund and Trust Fund.
18.For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010).
19.While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor establish or create GAD Focal Point in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW and prepare GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to properly allocate funds for the plans, programs and activities of the Municipality that address gender issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of women and men and by given into consideration the different characteristics, roles and expectations attributed by society to women and men.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the municipal accountant to prepare the reports on the unremitted retirement and life insurance premiums contributions of municipal officials and employees and the agency’ share to establish the reliable and accurate liability of the municipal government to GSIS and the amount to be recognized as Due to GSIS.

1.We recommended that the Honorable Mayor coordinate with the previous administration of the Municipality to submit reports and documents pertaining to the loan granted to the municipality, if any. Then turn over the equipment purchased from the proceeds of the loan so that the accounting office, general services office and other concerned offices can make the appropriate reporting and recording of the loan and the heavy equipment and proper recording and accounting in the Books of Accounts can be made thereof in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.
4.Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended LDRM Fund which represents the Quick Response Fund (QRF) from General Fund to Special Trust Fund.

5.For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010).

6.While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

8.We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the municipal accountant to prepare the reports on the unremitted retirement and life insurance premiums contributions of municipal officials and employees and the agency’ share to establish the reliable and accurate liability of the municipal government to GSIS as Due to GSIS.
10.Submit Contract of Service and Job Descriptions/Specifications of contractual or casual employees and observe the following measures:
11.Consider and limit the hiring of job orders to extremely necessary work assignment to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenses by the municipal government, and
12.Attached to the Daily Wage Payroll copy of the Contract of Service stipulated therein the specific purpose, work assignment and the specific duties and responsibilities of the job order employees.

15.Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records and deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers.
16.After physical count and report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment which should bear a Property Number.
17.The municipal accountant shall provide residual value in computing the depreciation of each asset. Obsolete and fully depreciated property account shal be transferred to Other Assets account.

18.We recommended that Management strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and submit to this office the following documents: Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP); and Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF

1.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require and supervise the Inventory Committee of the municipality
to conduct the physical count of PPE and update and submit the
RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor; and
b. Require the General Services Office to maintain Property Cards
and to reconcile their records on a regular monthly basis with the
Accounting Office.

2.5 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor create task force to
negotiate with the previous administration of the Municipality to turn
over the heavy equipment and settle the loans with the lending
institution. Then, upon proper turned over, the accounting office, general
services office and other concerned offices make the appropriate
reporting and recording of the loans and the proper accounts of the
heavy equipment in the agency’s Books of Accounts in accordance with
generally accepted accounting principles.

3.5 We recommended that Management submit the following required
documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and audit, viz:
a. Supplemental Budget and the Supplemental Annual Investment
Plan supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution/Ordinance;
and
b. Lastly, submit the list/names of beneficiaries of relief goods, if any,
to support proof of distribution of welfare goods expenses.

4.6 We recommended that Management:
a. Refrain from granting cash advances without first liquidating the
previous cash advance; and
b. Ensure that the documentary requirements prescribed under
Circular 2012-001 be properly complied with.

5.4 We reiterated and recommended that Management submit Contract of
Service and Job Descriptions/Specifications of contractual or casual
employees and observe the following measures:
a. Consider and limit the hiring of job orders to extremely necessary
work assignment to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenses
by the municipal government;
b. Regularly attach copies of the approved contracts of service to the
Daily Wage Payroll; and
c. Finally, cause the immediate submission of copies of contracts of
service of all contractual/casual and job order personnel.

7.3 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe and practice
the stamping of “PAID” stamped to every Disbursement Vouchers and
all its supporting documents after payments were made and indicate
therein the number of check issued and the date of the actual payment.

8.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to
post a tarpaulin signboard which must be suitably framed outdoor
display at the project location or its activities with all the information as
provided in Annex A (attached for ready reference) pursuant to
paragraph 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

9.4 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor require/instruct the
responsible/liable persons to submit the required documents or lacking
supporting papers within 90 days from receipt of an NS, otherwise, such
suspensions will become disallowance and settlement thereof will be
immediately enforced upon issuance of the Certificate of Execution by
withholding the salaries or other claims due the persons liable in
satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and executory.

2.We recommended that the Honorable Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and/or the Municipal Accountant to submit the Purchase Orders and Contracts of all procurements of goods and services to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from their execution. Disbursement Vouchers for the payments of the procurements shall be submitted on time to make the necessary review thereof pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001.

3.We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned agency officials to observe the fundamental principles provided under Section 4 of PD 1445 and other pertinent regulations under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated July 4, 2012 on the disbursements pertaining to the training and seminar expenses of the agency charged to DRRM Funds.

7.We recommended that the Honorable Mayor establish or create GAD Focal Point in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW and prepare GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to properly allocate funds for the plans, programs and activities of the Municipality that address gender issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of women and men and by given into consideration the different characteristics, roles and expectations attributed by society to women and men.

9.We recommended the Honorable Mayor for their compliance on tax rules and regulations in withholding the income tax from the basic salaries of the municipal officials and employees and remitting to the Bureau of Internal Revenue the exact amount deducted and also remitting the 5% VAT of their procurements.
13.We recommended that Management create an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment.
14.Prepare annual Reports on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

We recommended that Management strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and submit to this office the following documents:
a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP), and
b. Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor coordinate with the
previous administration of the Municipality to submit reports and
documents pertaining to the loan granted to the municipality, if any.
Consequently, the previous administration will make the proper turn-
over of the equipment purchased from the proceeds of the loan so that
the accounting office, general services office and other concerned
offices can make the appropriate reporting and recording of the loan as
well as the heavy equipment in the Books of Accounts in accordance
with generally accepted accounting principles.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer and/or the Municipal Accountant to submit the Purchase
Orders and Contracts of all procurements of goods and services to the
Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from their execution.
Disbursement Vouchers for the payments of the procurements shall be
submitted on time to make the necessary review thereof pursuant to
COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned
agency officials to observe the fundamental principles provided under
Section 4 of PD 1445 and other pertinent regulations under COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated July 4, 2012 on the disbursements
pertaining to the training and seminar expenses of the agency
charged to DRRM Funds.

We recommended that Honorable Mayor:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended
LDRRM Fund which represents the Quick Response Fund (QRF)
from General Fund to Special Trust Fund.
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of
unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher
taking the appropriate accounting entries under General Fund
and Trust Fund.

i. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current
Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and
credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account
(Account Code 1-01-02-010).
ii. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local
Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the
account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor establish or create GAD
Focal Point in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM-NEDA-
NCRFW and prepare GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to properly allocate
funds for the plans, programs and activities of the Municipality that
address gender issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of
women and men and by given into consideration the different
characteristics, roles and expectations attributed by society to women
and men.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the reports on the unremitted retirement and
life insurance premiums contributions of municipal officials and
employees and the agency’s share to establish the reliable and accurate
liability of the municipal government to GSIS and the amount to be
recognized as Due to GSIS.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor require the municipal
employees responsible/liable for the suspended/disallowed transactions
to submit the required documents within 90 days from receipt thereof.
Otherwise, it will be disallowed in audit and settlement thereof should be
immediately enforced by withholding the salaries or other claims due the
persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and
executory.

We recommended that Management shall:
1. submit Contract of Service and Job Descriptions/Specifications of Contractual and Casual employees and observe the following measures:
a. Consider and limit the hiring of job orders to extremely necessary work assignment to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenses by the municipal government, and
b. Attached to the Daily Wage Payroll copy of the Contract of Service stipulated therein the specific purpose, work assignment and the specific duties and responsibilities of the job order employees.

We recommended that Management instruct the Office of the Accountant to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) of its Depository Bank Account with the Philippine National Bank, Marawi Branch, Marawi City and submit it monthly together with the supporting documents such as original copies of bank statements, debit/credit memos (DM/CM) and paid checks to the Office of the Auditor as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management require the Accountable Officer to record promptly all checks issued in the cashbook so that it can be properly accounted for easy reconciliation of the same could be made.
4.We further recommended that Management initiate to improve the system of recording transactions with the use of checks issued in numerical sequence or in chronological order as reference pursuant to Section 41 of NGAs Manual, Volume I.

6. We recommended that Management create an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment.
7. Prepare annual Reports on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
8. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers.
9. After physical count and report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment which should bear a Property Number.
10. The Municipal accountant shall provide residual value in computing the depreciation of each asset. Obsolete and fully depreciated property account shall be transferred to Other Assets account.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal person to facilitate the formulation of GAD Plans and Budget (GPB) and ensure that activities identified as GAD issues and concerns are properly addressed pursuant to the provisions under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 and require Management to submit the Accomplishment Report on GAD Activities as required under the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to record promptly all collections in the cashbook-Cash in Treasury and the Municipal Accountant record the RPT collection to its Subsidiary Ledger pursuant to Section 13 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I for proper accountability,monitoring and verification.
13. We also recommended that Management improve the system of recording of income and revenues to the agency’s books of accounts and its prompt deposits to the agency authorized depository bank and issuance of official receipts must be in numerical sequence or in chronological order in compliance with accounting system for collections.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the annual SCBAA for the year ended December 31, 2018 to complete the submission of the financial statements at year end pursuant to IPSASs.

We recommended that the local Chief Executive shall compel all regular Municipal officials and employees appointed or elected including contractual employees to file or update their membership in the GSIS, PHILHEALTH and PAG-IBIG offices and deduct from their monthly their monthly contributions and remit with the respective GOCC’s in compliance to GSIS Act and R.A. 7742.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare break down of the lists of the
names and amount of the Inter Agency Payables and prepare aging of accounts payable of the LGU’s to establish
the reliability and accuracy of the Inter Agency Payables amounting to P 405,887.32 as of December 31, 2017 and
submit schedule and aging of Accounts Payables to the assigned audit team for verification.
observations.reason: No records and reports submitted on the inter agency payables and aging of accounts payable were provided.

observations.recommendation: We re-iterate our recommendation to the Management to create an Inventory Team (IT) to conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipal government at least once every end of the year to establish the most reliable value of the fixed assets of the municipal hence re-submit RPCPPE of the government to the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: Submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year.
observations.reason: No inventory of PPEs submitted for 2017; re-iterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
1.4. We recommended that Management submit the Contract of Service and
Job Descriptions/Specifications of contractual or casual employees and
observe the following measures:

a. Consider and limit the hiring of job orders to extremely necessary
work assignment to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenses
by the municipal government;
b. Attached to the Daily Wage Payroll copy of the Contract of Service
stipulated therein the specific purpose, work assignment and the specific
duties and responsibilities of the job order employees.

2.5. We recommended that Management instruct the Office of the Accountant
to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) of its Depository Bank
Account with the Philippine National Bank, Marawi Branch, Marawi
City and submit it monthly together with the supporting documents such as original copies of bank statements, debit/credit memos (DM/CM) and
paid checks to the Office of the Auditor as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

3.6. We recommended that Management require the Accountable Officer to
record promptly all checks issued in the cashbook so that it can be
properly accounted for easy reconciliation of the same could be made.

3.7. We further recommended that Management initiate to improve the system of
recording transactions with the use of checks issued in numerical sequence or in
chronological order as reference pursuant to Section 41 of NGAs Manual,
Volume I.

4.4. We recommended that Management strictly follow the rules and
regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12,
2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and submit to
this office the following documents:

a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP), and the
b. Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis

5.10. We recommended that Management create an appraisal/inventory
committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties,
plant and equipment and prepare annual Reports on Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Also direct the Municipal
Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies
noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the
inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers. After physical count and
report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger
card on each equipment which should bear a Property Number. The
municipal accountant shall provide residual value in computing the
depreciation of each asset. Obsolete and fully depreciated property
account shall be transferred to Other Assets account.

6.5. We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal person to
facilitate the formulation of GAD Plans and Budget (GPB) and ensure
that activities identified as GAD issues and concerns are properly
addressed pursuant to the provisions under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular 2014-001
dated March 18, 2014 and require management to submit the
Accomplishment Report on GAD Activities as required under the
aforementioned regulations.

7.5. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to
record promptly all collections in the cashbook-Cash in Treasury and the
Municipal Accountant also record the RPT collection to its Subsidiary
Ledger pursuant to Section 13 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I for
proper accountability, monitoring and verification.

7.6. We also recommended that Management improve the system of recording
of income and revenues to the agency’s books of accounts and its prompt
deposits to the agency authorized depository bank and issuance of official
receipts must be in numerical sequence or in chronological order in
compliance with accounting system for collections.

9.2. We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and submit the annual SCBAA for the year ended December 31,
2018 to complete the submission of the financial statements at year end
pursuant to IPSASs.

10.7. We recommended that the local Chief Executive shall compel all regular
municipal officials and employees appointed or elected including
contractual employees to file or update their membership in the
GSIS, PHILHEALTH and PAG-IBIG offices and deduct from their
monthly their monthly contributions and remit with the respective
GOCC’s in compliance to GSIS Act and R.A. 7742.

We recommend that management submit on the prescribed
Liquidation Reports of the fund transfer submitted on time pursuant to Section 3.1.3 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 otherwise known as the Revised
Documentary Requirements For Common Government Transactions to give ample time for the
audit team to conduct audit and verification of the transactions and prepare audit action
thereon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant shall furnish the audit team the loan documents and submit up-dated Statement of Accounts at the end of the year to the Office of the Auditor in order for the audit team to have documents pertaining to the loans and in order for the audit team to have verification on the amount of amortization and interest expenses debited on the bank statements and reconcile with the agency records. Keep a complete file of the loan documents for ready reference and verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit on the prescribed period the Liquidation Reports of the fund transfer supported with the Reports of Checks Issued and Reports of disbursements duly certified by the Municipal Accountant and approved by the agency head pursuant to Section 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 otherwise known as the Revised Documentary Requirements For Common Government Transactions to give ample time for the audit team to conduct audit and verification of the transactions and prepare audit action thereon.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reports on the monthly interest expense, insurance  and other financial expenses related to the loans which were debited by the bank from the LGU’s account and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and record/post to the corresponding ledgers and journals. Maintain Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations – Financial Expenses (RAAOFE) as required under Section 22 of NGAS for LGU Volume II to establish the actual amount of interest expense and other financial expenses at the end of the period.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare break down of the lists of the names and amount of Inter Agency Payables  and prepare aging of accounts payable of LGU’s to establish the reliability and accuracy of the Inter Agency Payables  amounting to P 405,887.32 as of December 31, 2017 and submit schedule and aging of Accounts Payables to the assigned audit team for verification.

We re-iterate our recommendation to the Management to create an Inventory Team (IT) to conduct the physical count of the Property,  Plant and Equipment of the municipal government at least once every end of the year to establish the most reliable value of the fixed assets of the municipal government in compliance to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. Submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to up-date the Subsidiary Ledgers of all the agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment and compute the monthly depreciation based on the regulations provided under COA Circular No. 2003-007.
Also require the Municipal Accountant to record all items of property, plant and equipment in accordance with PPSAS  No. 17 and to draw a journal entry voucher to effect the adjusting and correcting entries on the misstated Accumulated Depreciation and the Depreciation Expenses accounts for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statement during the end of the period.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer	(MDRRMO) to prepare actual programs, projects, activities and expenditures pertaining to the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the latter to prepare the agency’s Quarterly Report on the Source and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 	12, 2012.

We recommend that Management create Local Solid Waste Management Board to formulate 	and prepare plans and programs on the solid waste management of the municipality to promote public health and sanitation and environment awareness in compliance to Sections 12 and 16 of R.A. 9003.

We recommend that management submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget for 2017 to allow the disbursement charged to GAD funds in compliance with Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW. The  head of each department constitute as focal Point System of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of the plans and programs related to GAD Mainstreaming. Furthermore, set GAD agenda for 3-year period and prioritized the gender issues to be addressed by the municipal government.

We recommend that Management direct the officials concerned to submit within 90 days the required and/or lacking supporting documents on  the  transactions  suspended  in  audit, otherwise it will be disallowed and Notice of Finality of Decision is  issued to effect withholding of salaries or other claims due the persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts disallowed.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual.  Likewise, turn over the receipts and the  disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents in compliance with Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
          Require the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports, and to facilitate the recording of the transaction in the books of accounts.
Advice the Municipal Treasurer to pay only disbursement vouchers with the complete documents pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.reason: Not implement-ted | Municipal  accountant no sufficient experience to handle accounting works

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and/or the Municipal Accountant shall furnish the audit team the loan documents and submit up-dated Statement of Accounts as of the December 31, 2015 to the Office of the Auditor in order for the audit team to have documents pertaining to the loans and in order for the audit team to have verification on the amount of amortization and interest expenses debited on the bank statements and reconcile with the agency records. Keep a complete file of the loan documents for ready reference and verification.
observations.reason: No action | No committee created to conduct inventory of PPE

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the monthly interest expenses of  loans payable debited by the bank from the LGU’s account and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and record/post to the corresponding ledgers and journals. Maintain Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations – Financial Expenses (RAAOFE) as required under Section 22 of NGAS for LGU Volume II to establish the actual amount of interest expense at the end of the period.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare aging of accounts payable of LGU’s and make lists of the names and amount of payables to LGU to establish the reliability and accuracy of the Due to LGU account amounting to P 28,762.50 as of December 31, 2016 and submit schedule and aging of Accounts Payables to the assigned audit team for verification

Management shall adhered strictly  sections 111 and 112 of P.D. 1445 which mandated the keeping of accounts and the recording of transactions respectively. It specifically states that:
Section 111.  Keeping of account. (1) The accounts of the agency shall be kept in 	such detail as it necessary to meet the needs of the agency and at the same time the adequate to furnish the information needed by the fiscal  or control agencies of the government. (2) the highest standards of honesty, objectivity and consistency shall be observed in the keeping of accounts to safeguard against inaccurate or 	misleading information.
Section 112. Recording of financial transaction. Each government agency shall records its financial transactions and operations conformably with generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

Create a team to conduct the physical count of the Property,  Plant and Equipment of the municipal government at the end of the year to establish the most reliable value of the  fixed assets of the municipal government in compliance to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

Direct  the Municipal Accountant to up-date the Subsidiary Ledgers of all the agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment and compute the monthly depreciation based on the regulations provided under COA Circular No. 2003-007.

Also require the Municipal Accountant to record all items of property, plant and equipment at cost in accordance with the International Accounting Standards No. 16 and to draw a journal entry voucher to effect the adjusting and correcting entries of the misstated Accumulated Depreciation Account and the Depreciation Expense Accounts.

Create Local Solid Waste Management Board to formulate and prepare plans and programs on the solid waste management of the municipality to promote public health and sanitation and promote environment awareness in compliance to Sections 12 and 16 of R.A. 9003.

Pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW, head of each department shall constitute as focal Point System of the Municipality to facilitate the institutionalization of Gender Mainstreaming. Furthermore, set  GAD agenda for 3-year period and prioritized the gender issues to be addressed by the municipal government. Focal Point System formulates GAD plans, programs, projects and activities to be integrated in the GAD Plan and Budget in order to be compliant of the rules and regulations provided in the Republic Act 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women.

The officials concerned were advised to comply within 90 days with the requirements of   the  transactions  suspended  in  audit  and  submit the  lacking supporting papers. Otherwise, it will be disallowed in audit and Settlement thereof should be immediately enforced by withholding the salaries or other claims due the persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and executory.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual.  Likewise, turn over the receipts and the  disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents in compliance with Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
          Require the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports, and to facilitate the recording of the transaction in the books of accounts.
Advice the Municipal Treasurer to pay only disbursement vouchers with the complete documents pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

Keep a complete file of the loan documents for ready reference and verification.
